dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9348 IH: Public Access Protection Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Friedman introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure

form.official-title: To require the Secretary of the Army to include in any final recommendation for a water resources development project of the Corps of Engineers a plan to maintain equivalent levels of access to existing public recreational amenities, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Public Access Protection Act.

It is the sense of Congress that, during construction of a water resources development project of the Corps of Engineers that affects a public recreational amenity, including a park, a trail, a green space, a recreational waterway, and any other public open space, the Secretary of the Army should—

paragraph.text: maintain an equivalent level of recreational access to such public recreational amenity;
minimize temporary disruptions to such access through project planning and coordination with affected communities; and
provide alternative public access where necessary to ensure continued recreational opportunities for the duration of such construction.

section.text: To the maximum extent practicable, in carrying out a feasibility study for a water resources development project of the Corps of Engineers, the Secretary shall—

paragraph.text: review the potential impact of construction of the project on existing public recreational amenities, including facilities for hiking, biking, walking, and waterborne recreation; and
include in any final recommendation for such project a plan to maintain an equivalent level of access to such amenities during construction of the project.
